During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation Needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |Clan name= Kimotsuki clan |Crest= Satsuma - Kimotsuke.svg |Crest name= Opposing Tsuru feeding Wakamatsu
Three Mitsu Karigane |Original surname= [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Tomo]] [[Sukune]] |Founder= [[Tomo no Kanetoshi|Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] |Type= [[Samurai clan|Samurai]]
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] - main line
[[Kazoku]] (baron) - branch line |Homeland= [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] [[Kimotsuki District]]{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1959}} |Base of operations= Ōsumi Province, Kimotsuki District |Prominent figures= [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]]
[[Komatsu Kiyosuke]]
[[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanemasa]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] |Branches= [[Kitahara clan]] (samurai)
[[Yakumaru clan]] (samurai)
[[Anraku clan]] (samurai)
[[Umekita clan]] (samurai)
[[Ei clan]] (samurai)
Ogihara clan (samurai)
[[Izumi clan]] (samurai)
[[Asokuma clan]] (samurai)
Kimotsuki Baronial Family (samurai, baron) }} '''Kimotsuki clan''' (きもつきし) is one of the [[clans of Japan]], which emerged as a [[sengoku daimyo]] of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku era]]. They fiercely contested territory with their neighboring [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] but ultimately became vassals of the Shimazu. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the clan persisted as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the main line of the clan became [[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]], while the branch family of [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]], who rose to the Rank of [[Vice Admiral (Imperial Japanese Navy)|Vice Admiral]] in the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], was awarded the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the [[kazoku]] peerage for his achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. __TOC__{{-}} == History == The clan’s original surname was [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Tomo clan]]. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[Tomo no Kaneyuki]] (a great-grandson of [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], the son of Yoshio → Nakayō → Nakakane → Kanetō → Kaneyuki) was appointed deputy governor of [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] and settled in the region. Kaneyuki’s son, [[Tomo no Yukisada|Yukisada]], and Yukisada’s son, [[Tomo no Kanesada|Kanesada]] (whose wife was the daughter of [[Taira no Suemochi]], the developer of [[Shimazu Manor]] or the daughter of Suemochi’s son, Kanesuke), became the compensation commissioner of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]], Kimotsuki District. During the tenure of Kanesada’s son [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi|Kanetoshi]], the family adopted the name '''Kimotsuki''' (肝属) derived from the district name. By [[:en:1036|1036]] (9th year of [[Chōgen era]]), the Kimotsuki clan had already established residence in the region.The year provided follows the Kimotsuki town official website’s "Yabusame Festival" ([http://kimotsuki-town.jp/kimotsuki03/kimotsuki07.html external link]). During the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Kimotsuki clan sided with the [[Southern Court (Japan)|Southern Court]] and fought against the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]]. After the conflicts settled, they submitted to the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. However, during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], disputes over territory led to tensions with the Shimazu clan. They aligned with the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] of [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] to oppose the Shimazu. The 16th clan leader, [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]], was a notable general who defeated [[Shimazu Tadaosa]] (brother of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]]) at the [[Battle of Takehara-yama]] and captured [[Shibushi District]]. For a time, the Kimotsuki clan gained the upper hand over the Shimazu. However, in the 8th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1566), they suffered a counterattack by the Shimazu army, leading Kanetsugu to commit suicide (though some sources dispute the suicide theory). This marked the clan’s rapid decline. The 18th head of the clan, [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]], sought revenge for his father’s death and resisted the Shimazu. However, in the first year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1573), internal conflicts among pro-Shimazu vassals, and opposition from his stepmother [[:en:Onami|Onami]] (sister of Shimazu Takahisa), led to his deposition. The 19th head, [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]], submitted to the Shimazu in the 2nd year of Tenshō (1574). While the family name survived, their territories were confiscated by the Shimazu in December of the 8th year of Tenshō (January 1581), reducing the Kimotsuki to mere vassals and marking the end of their status as daimyo. In the 5th year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] (1600), Kanemori was killed in action during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]]. His eldest son, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki|Kaneyuki]], drowned during a storm off [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] while accompanying [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] on a mission to bring [[:en:Shō Nei|Shō Nei]], the [[:en:King of Ryūkyū|King of Ryūkyū]], to [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. The family continued through adoption from the [[Niiro family]] of the Shimazu clan, maintaining their status as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. Branches of the Kimotsuki clan served the Shimazu early on, holding significant posts. During the Edo period, one branch became the lord of [[Kiire]], maintaining the [[Ittomochi]] (one estate) status with 5,500 koku. The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] or [[:en:Sadowara Domain|Sadowara Domain]]. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation Needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |Clan name= Kimotsuki clan |Crest= Satsuma - Kimotsuke.svg |Crest name= Opposing Tsuru feeding Wakamatsu

Kimotsuki Baronial Family (samurai, baron) }} '''Kimotsuki clan''' (きもつきし) is one of the [[clans of Japan]], which emerged as a [[sengoku daimyo]] of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku era]]. They fiercely contested territory with their neighboring [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] but ultimately became vassals of the Shimazu. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the clan persisted as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the main line of the clan became [[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]], while the branch family of [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]], who rose to the Rank of [[Vice Admiral (Imperial Japanese Navy)|Vice Admiral]] in the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], was awarded the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the [[kazoku]] peerage for his achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. __TOC__{{-}} == History == The clan’s original surname was [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Tomo clan]]. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[Tomo no Kaneyuki]] (a great-grandson of [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], the son of Yoshio → Nakayō → Nakakane → Kanetō → Kaneyuki) was appointed deputy governor of [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] and settled in the region. Kaneyuki’s son, [[Tomo no Yukisada|Yukisada]], and Yukisada’s son, [[Tomo no Kanesada|Kanesada]] (whose wife was the daughter of [[Taira no Suemochi]], the developer of [[Shimazu Manor]] or the daughter of Suemochi’s son, Kanesuke), became the compensation commissioner of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]], Kimotsuki District. During the tenure of Kanesada’s son [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi|Kanetoshi]], the family adopted the name '''Kimotsuki''' (肝属) derived from the district name. By [[:en:1036|1036]] (9th year of [[Chōgen era]]), the Kimotsuki clan had already established residence in the region.The year provided follows the Kimotsuki town official website’s "Yabusame Festival" ([http://kimotsuki-town.jp/kimotsuki03/kimotsuki07.html external link]). During the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Kimotsuki clan sided with the [[Southern Court (Japan)|Southern Court]] and fought against the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]]. After the conflicts settled, they submitted to the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. However, during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], disputes over territory led to tensions with the Shimazu clan. They aligned with the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] of [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] to oppose the Shimazu. The 16th clan leader, [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]], was a notable general who defeated [[Shimazu Tadaosa]] (brother of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]]) at the [[Battle of Takehara-yama]] and captured [[Shibushi District]]. For a time, the Kimotsuki clan gained the upper hand over the Shimazu. However, in the 8th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1566), they suffered a counterattack by the Shimazu army, leading Kanetsugu to commit suicide (though some sources dispute the suicide theory). This marked the clan’s rapid decline. The 18th head of the clan, [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]], sought revenge for his father’s death and resisted the Shimazu. However, in the first year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1573), internal conflicts among pro-Shimazu vassals, and opposition from his stepmother [[:en:Onami|Onami]] (sister of Shimazu Takahisa), led to his deposition. The 19th head, [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]], submitted to the Shimazu in the 2nd year of Tenshō (1574). While the family name survived, their territories were confiscated by the Shimazu in December of the 8th year of Tenshō (January 1581), reducing the Kimotsuki to mere vassals and marking the end of their status as daimyo. In the 5th year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] (1600), Kanemori was killed in action during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]]. His eldest son, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki|Kaneyuki]], drowned during a storm off [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] while accompanying [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] on a mission to bring [[:en:Shō Nei|Shō Nei]], the [[:en:King of Ryūkyū|King of Ryūkyū]], to [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. The family continued through adoption from the [[Niiro family]] of the Shimazu clan, maintaining their status as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. Branches of the Kimotsuki clan served the Shimazu early on, holding significant posts. During the Edo period, one branch became the lord of [[Kiire]], maintaining the [[Ittomochi]] (one estate) status with 5,500 koku. The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] or [[:en:Sadowara Domain|Sadowara Domain]]. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation Needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |Clan name= Kimotsuki clan |Crest= Satsuma - Kimotsuke.svg |Crest name= Opposing Tsuru feeding Wakamatsu

Kimotsuki Baronial Family (samurai, baron) }} '''Kimotsuki clan''' (きもつきし) is one of the [[clans of Japan]], which emerged as a [[sengoku daimyo]] of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku era]]. They fiercely contested territory with their neighboring [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] but ultimately became vassals of the Shimazu. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the clan persisted as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the main line of the clan became [[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]], while the branch family of [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]], who rose to the Rank of [[Vice Admiral (Imperial Japanese Navy)|Vice Admiral]] in the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], was awarded the title of [[:en:Baron|Baron]] in the [[kazoku]] peerage for his achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. __TOC__{{-}} == History == The clan’s original surname was [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Tomo clan]]. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[Tomo no Kaneyuki]] (a great-grandson of [[:en:Tomo no Yoshio|Tomo no Yoshio]], the son of Yoshio → Nakayō → Nakakane → Kanetō → Kaneyuki) was appointed deputy governor of [[:en:Satsuma Province|Satsuma Province]] and settled in the region. Kaneyuki’s son, [[Tomo no Yukisada|Yukisada]], and Yukisada’s son, [[Tomo no Kanesada|Kanesada]] (whose wife was the daughter of [[Taira no Suemochi]], the developer of [[Shimazu Manor]] or the daughter of Suemochi’s son, Kanesuke), became the compensation commissioner of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]], Kimotsuki District. During the tenure of Kanesada’s son [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi|Kanetoshi]], the family adopted the name '''Kimotsuki''' (肝属) derived from the district name. By [[:en:1036|1036]] (9th year of [[Chōgen era]]), the Kimotsuki clan had already established residence in the region.The year provided follows the Kimotsuki town official website’s "Yabusame Festival" ([http://kimotsuki-town.jp/kimotsuki03/kimotsuki07.html external link]). During the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]], the Kimotsuki clan sided with the [[Southern Court (Japan)|Southern Court]] and fought against the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]]. After the conflicts settled, they submitted to the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. However, during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], disputes over territory led to tensions with the Shimazu clan. They aligned with the [[:en:Itō clan|Itō clan]] of [[:en:Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] to oppose the Shimazu. The 16th clan leader, [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]], was a notable general who defeated [[Shimazu Tadaosa]] (brother of [[:en:Shimazu Takahisa|Shimazu Takahisa]]) at the [[Battle of Takehara-yama]] and captured [[Shibushi District]]. For a time, the Kimotsuki clan gained the upper hand over the Shimazu. However, in the 8th year of [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] (1566), they suffered a counterattack by the Shimazu army, leading Kanetsugu to commit suicide (though some sources dispute the suicide theory). This marked the clan’s rapid decline. The 18th head of the clan, [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]], sought revenge for his father’s death and resisted the Shimazu. However, in the first year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1573), internal conflicts among pro-Shimazu vassals, and opposition from his stepmother [[:en:Onami|Onami]] (sister of Shimazu Takahisa), led to his deposition. The 19th head, [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]], submitted to the Shimazu in the 2nd year of Tenshō (1574). While the family name survived, their territories were confiscated by the Shimazu in December of the 8th year of Tenshō (January 1581), reducing the Kimotsuki to mere vassals and marking the end of their status as daimyo. In the 5th year of [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] (1600), Kanemori was killed in action during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]]. His eldest son, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki|Kaneyuki]], drowned during a storm off [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] while accompanying [[:en:Shimazu Tadatsune|Shimazu Tadatsune]] on a mission to bring [[:en:Shō Nei|Shō Nei]], the [[:en:King of Ryūkyū|King of Ryūkyū]], to [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. The family continued through adoption from the [[Niiro family]] of the Shimazu clan, maintaining their status as retainers of the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]]. Branches of the Kimotsuki clan served the Shimazu early on, holding significant posts. During the Edo period, one branch became the lord of [[Kiire]], maintaining the [[Ittomochi]] (one estate) status with 5,500 koku. The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the [[:en:Satsuma Domain|Satsuma Domain]] or [[:en:Sadowara Domain|Sadowara Domain]]. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # [[:en:Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, [[:en:Mandolin|Mandolin]] Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611486]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation Needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |Clan name= Kimotsuki clan |Crest= Satsuma - Kimotsuke.svg |Crest name= Opposing Tsuru feeding Wakamatsu
Three Mitsu Karigane |Original surname= {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Tomo}} [[Sukune]] |Founder= [[Tomo no Kanetoshi|Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] |Type= [[Samurai clan|Samurai]]
{{ill|Shizoku|en|Shizoku|WD=Q3482406|lt=Shizoku}} - main line
[[Kazoku]] (baron) - branch line |Homeland= {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} [[Kimotsuki District]]{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1959}} |Base of operations= Ōsumi Province, Kimotsuki District |Prominent figures= {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}}
[[Komatsu Kiyosuke]]
[[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanemasa]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] |Branches= [[Kitahara clan]] (samurai)
[[Yakumaru clan]] (samurai)
[[Anraku clan]] (samurai)
[[Umekita clan]] (samurai)
[[Ei clan]] (samurai)
Ogihara clan (samurai)
[[Izumi clan]] (samurai)
[[Asokuma clan]] (samurai)
Kimotsuki Baronial Family (samurai, baron) }} '''Kimotsuki clan''' (きもつきし) is one of the [[clans of Japan]], which emerged as a [[sengoku daimyo]] of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku era]]. They fiercely contested territory with their neighboring {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}} but ultimately became vassals of the Shimazu. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the clan persisted as retainers of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the main line of the clan became {{ill|Shizoku|en|Shizoku|WD=Q3482406|lt=Shizoku}}, while the branch family of [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]], who rose to the Rank of [[Vice Admiral (Imperial Japanese Navy)|Vice Admiral]] in the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy}}, was awarded the title of {{ill|Baron|en|Baron|WD=Q165503|lt=Baron}} in the [[kazoku]] peerage for his achievements in the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. __TOC__{{-}} == History == The clan’s original surname was {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Tomo clan}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[Tomo no Kaneyuki]] (a great-grandson of {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio}}, the son of Yoshio → Nakayō → Nakakane → Kanetō → Kaneyuki) was appointed deputy governor of {{ill|Satsuma Province|en|Satsuma Province|WD=Q667475|lt=Satsuma Province}} and settled in the region. Kaneyuki’s son, [[Tomo no Yukisada|Yukisada]], and Yukisada’s son, [[Tomo no Kanesada|Kanesada]] (whose wife was the daughter of [[Taira no Suemochi]], the developer of [[Shimazu Manor]] or the daughter of Suemochi’s son, Kanesuke), became the compensation commissioner of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}}, Kimotsuki District. During the tenure of Kanesada’s son [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi|Kanetoshi]], the family adopted the name '''Kimotsuki''' (肝属) derived from the district name. By {{ill|1036|en|1036|WD=Q19328|lt=1036}} (9th year of [[Chōgen era]]), the Kimotsuki clan had already established residence in the region.The year provided follows the Kimotsuki town official website’s "Yabusame Festival" ([http://kimotsuki-town.jp/kimotsuki03/kimotsuki07.html external link]). During the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the Kimotsuki clan sided with the [[Southern Court (Japan)|Southern Court]] and fought against the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]]. After the conflicts settled, they submitted to the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. However, during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], disputes over territory led to tensions with the Shimazu clan. They aligned with the {{ill|Itō clan|en|Itō clan|WD=Q2975280|lt=Itō clan}} of {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|WD=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province}} to oppose the Shimazu. The 16th clan leader, {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}}, was a notable general who defeated [[Shimazu Tadaosa]] (brother of {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|en|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|lt=Shimazu Takahisa}}) at the [[Battle of Takehara-yama]] and captured [[Shibushi District]]. For a time, the Kimotsuki clan gained the upper hand over the Shimazu. However, in the 8th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1566), they suffered a counterattack by the Shimazu army, leading Kanetsugu to commit suicide (though some sources dispute the suicide theory). This marked the clan’s rapid decline. The 18th head of the clan, [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]], sought revenge for his father’s death and resisted the Shimazu. However, in the first year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1573), internal conflicts among pro-Shimazu vassals, and opposition from his stepmother [[:en:Onami|Onami]] (sister of Shimazu Takahisa), led to his deposition. The 19th head, [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]], submitted to the Shimazu in the 2nd year of Tenshō (1574). While the family name survived, their territories were confiscated by the Shimazu in December of the 8th year of Tenshō (January 1581), reducing the Kimotsuki to mere vassals and marking the end of their status as daimyo. In the 5th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} (1600), Kanemori was killed in action during the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}}. His eldest son, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki|Kaneyuki]], drowned during a storm off {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province}} while accompanying {{ill|Shimazu Tadatsune|en|Shimazu Tadatsune|WD=Q1185372|lt=Shimazu Tadatsune}} on a mission to bring {{ill|Shō Nei|en|Shō Nei|WD=Q907317|lt=Shō Nei}}, the [[:en:King of Ryūkyū|King of Ryūkyū]], to {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}}. The family continued through adoption from the [[Niiro family]] of the Shimazu clan, maintaining their status as retainers of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}. Branches of the Kimotsuki clan served the Shimazu early on, holding significant posts. During the Edo period, one branch became the lord of [[Kiire]], maintaining the [[Ittomochi]] (one estate) status with 5,500 koku. The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}} or {{ill|Sadowara Domain|en|Sadowara Domain|WD=Q10863373|lt=Sadowara Domain}}. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, {{ill|Mandolin|en|Mandolin|WD=Q302497|lt=Mandolin}} Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}} or {{ill|Sadowara Domain|en|Sadowara Domain|WD=Q10863373|lt=Sadowara Domain}}. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, {{ill|Mandolin|en|Mandolin|WD=Q302497|lt=Mandolin}} Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]][[ja:肝付氏]][[zh:肝付氏]] {{Citation Needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |Clan name= Kimotsuki clan |Crest= Satsuma - Kimotsuke.svg |Crest name= Opposing Tsuru feeding Wakamatsu
Three Mitsu Karigane |Original surname= {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Tomo}} [[Sukune]] |Founder= [[Tomo no Kanetoshi|Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] |Type= [[Samurai clan|Samurai]]
{{ill|Shizoku|en|Shizoku|WD=Q3482406|lt=Shizoku}} - main line
[[Kazoku]] (baron) - branch line |Homeland= {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} [[Kimotsuki District]]{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1959}} |Base of operations= Ōsumi Province, Kimotsuki District |Prominent figures= {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}}
[[Komatsu Kiyosuke]]
[[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanemasa]]
[[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] |Branches= [[Kitahara clan]] (samurai)
[[Yakumaru clan]] (samurai)
[[Anraku clan]] (samurai)
[[Umekita clan]] (samurai)
[[Ei clan]] (samurai)
Ogihara clan (samurai)
[[Izumi clan]] (samurai)
[[Asokuma clan]] (samurai)
Kimotsuki Baronial Family (samurai, baron) }} '''Kimotsuki clan''' (きもつきし) is one of the [[clans of Japan]], which emerged as a [[sengoku daimyo]] of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku era]]. They fiercely contested territory with their neighboring {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}} but ultimately became vassals of the Shimazu. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the clan persisted as retainers of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the main line of the clan became {{ill|Shizoku|en|Shizoku|WD=Q3482406|lt=Shizoku}}, while the branch family of [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]], who rose to the Rank of [[Vice Admiral (Imperial Japanese Navy)|Vice Admiral]] in the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy}}, was awarded the title of {{ill|Baron|en|Baron|WD=Q165503|lt=Baron}} in the [[kazoku]] peerage for his achievements in the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. __TOC__{{-}} == History == The clan’s original surname was {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Tomo clan}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[Tomo no Kaneyuki]] (a great-grandson of {{ill|Tomo no Yoshio|en|Tomo no Yoshio|WD=Q7820079|lt=Tomo no Yoshio}}, the son of Yoshio → Nakayō → Nakakane → Kanetō → Kaneyuki) was appointed deputy governor of {{ill|Satsuma Province|en|Satsuma Province|WD=Q667475|lt=Satsuma Province}} and settled in the region. Kaneyuki’s son, [[Tomo no Yukisada|Yukisada]], and Yukisada’s son, [[Tomo no Kanesada|Kanesada]] (whose wife was the daughter of [[Taira no Suemochi]], the developer of [[Shimazu Manor]] or the daughter of Suemochi’s son, Kanesuke), became the compensation commissioner of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}}, Kimotsuki District. During the tenure of Kanesada’s son [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi|Kanetoshi]], the family adopted the name '''Kimotsuki''' (肝属) derived from the district name. By {{ill|1036|en|1036|WD=Q19328|lt=1036}} (9th year of [[Chōgen era]]), the Kimotsuki clan had already established residence in the region.The year provided follows the Kimotsuki town official website’s "Yabusame Festival" ([http://kimotsuki-town.jp/kimotsuki03/kimotsuki07.html external link]). During the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the Kimotsuki clan sided with the [[Southern Court (Japan)|Southern Court]] and fought against the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]]. After the conflicts settled, they submitted to the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. However, during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], disputes over territory led to tensions with the Shimazu clan. They aligned with the {{ill|Itō clan|en|Itō clan|WD=Q2975280|lt=Itō clan}} of {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|WD=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province}} to oppose the Shimazu. The 16th clan leader, {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}}, was a notable general who defeated [[Shimazu Tadaosa]] (brother of {{ill|Shimazu Takahisa|en|Shimazu Takahisa|WD=Q2367308|lt=Shimazu Takahisa}}) at the [[Battle of Takehara-yama]] and captured [[Shibushi District]]. For a time, the Kimotsuki clan gained the upper hand over the Shimazu. However, in the 8th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} (1566), they suffered a counterattack by the Shimazu army, leading Kanetsugu to commit suicide (though some sources dispute the suicide theory). This marked the clan’s rapid decline. The 18th head of the clan, [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]], sought revenge for his father’s death and resisted the Shimazu. However, in the first year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1573), internal conflicts among pro-Shimazu vassals, and opposition from his stepmother [[:en:Onami|Onami]] (sister of Shimazu Takahisa), led to his deposition. The 19th head, [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]], submitted to the Shimazu in the 2nd year of Tenshō (1574). While the family name survived, their territories were confiscated by the Shimazu in December of the 8th year of Tenshō (January 1581), reducing the Kimotsuki to mere vassals and marking the end of their status as daimyo. In the 5th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} (1600), Kanemori was killed in action during the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}}. His eldest son, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki|Kaneyuki]], drowned during a storm off {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province}} while accompanying {{ill|Shimazu Tadatsune|en|Shimazu Tadatsune|WD=Q1185372|lt=Shimazu Tadatsune}} on a mission to bring {{ill|Shō Nei|en|Shō Nei|WD=Q907317|lt=Shō Nei}}, the [[:en:King of Ryūkyū|King of Ryūkyū]], to {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}}. The family continued through adoption from the [[Niiro family]] of the Shimazu clan, maintaining their status as retainers of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}. Branches of the Kimotsuki clan served the Shimazu early on, holding significant posts. During the Edo period, one branch became the lord of [[Kiire]], maintaining the [[Ittomochi]] (one estate) status with 5,500 koku. The [[Komatsu family]] branch produced the prominent [[Komatsu Kiyosuke]] (Komatsu Tatewaki). Other branches served as samurai for the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}} or {{ill|Sadowara Domain|en|Sadowara Domain|WD=Q10863373|lt=Sadowara Domain}}. From the branch line, [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] rose to the Rank of [[Imperial Japanese Navy Vice Admiral|Navy Vice Admiral]] in modern Japan and was ennobled as a baron for his wartime achievements in the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. The Kimotsuki clan only produced one noble family. Kaneyuki also served as [[Mayor of Osaka]] and a member of the [[:en:House of Peers|House of Peers]]{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. His son, [[Kimotsuki Kanehide|Kanehide]], who inherited the title, also served as a member of the House of Peers{{sfn|Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|1996|p=513}}. During Kanehide’s time, the Kimotsuki baronial family resided in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}’s [[Toshima Ward]], Horinouchi Town{{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Publishing Committee|1990|p=567}}. The voice actor [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] (real name: Kimotsuki Kanemasa) is a descendant of a Kimotsuki branch line.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140202205159/http://www.nhk.or.jp/furu-tabi/80chan/past/diary/20120305.html NHK Radio 1 "Here is Hometown Travel Radio" March 5, 2012 "Announcer’s Travel Diary"] (archived on February 2, 2014). == Heads of the Kimotsuki Clan == # [[Kimotsuki Kanetoshi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekyō]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanekazu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneishi]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanefuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneshige]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanejū]] # [[Kimotsuki Akiyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneuji]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemoto]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetada]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Kanenaga]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanehiro]] # {{ill|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|en|Kimotsuki Kanetsugu|WD=Q6410238|lt=Kimotsuki Kanetsugu}} # [[Kimotsuki Yoshitsugu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanetaka]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneyasu]] # [[Kimotsuki Kanechika]] # [[Kimotsuki Toshikane]] # [[Kimotsuki Tsunekane]] # [[Kimotsuki Kaneaki]] == Branches of the Kimotsuki Clan == * [[Kimotsuki Kanemitsu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanekuni]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetsune]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemori]] * [[Kimotsuki Kaneyuki]] - Navy Vice Admiral, 5th [[Mayor of Osaka]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanenobu]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanetada|Kimotsuki Kanetada (real name Kanemasa)]] - [[:en:Voice Actor|Voice Actor]] * [[Kimotsuki Kanemi]] - Musician, {{ill|Mandolin|en|Mandolin|WD=Q302497|lt=Mandolin}} Player == References == {{Citation Needed}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|Japanese|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|author-link=Ōta Ryō|others=[[Ueda Man'en]] and [[Mikami Sanji]] (eds.)|chapter=Kimotsuki Clan|pages=1959-1966|volume=2|date=1934|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Surnames and Family Trees|publisher=Surname and Family Tree Dictionary Publishing Committee|id={{National Bibliography Number|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/69}} National Diet Library Digital Collections|ref=harv}} {{Free Access}} * Military Family Chronicles: [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kimo_k.html Kimotsuki Clan] * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=1996|title=Revised Genealogy of Peer Families, Vol. 1|author=Kasumi Kaikan Peerage Genealogical Committee|publisher=[[Kasumi Kaikan]]|isbn=978-4642036702|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|date=2006|title=Peerage: The Illusions and Realities of Modern Japanese Aristocracy|author=Kodabe Yūji|authorlink=Kodabe Yūji|publisher=[[Chūō Kōronsha]]|series=[[Chūō Kōron New Books]] 1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|Japanese|date=2015|title=Dictionary of Petitioners for Peerage Titles|author=Matsuda Takayuki|authorlink=Matsuda Takayuki|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn= 978-4642014724|ref=harv}} == See Also == * [[Kōkai Sanryō]] - The imperial Kofuns designated by the Ministry of the Imperial Household in Meiji 7 (1874) in the ancestral territory of the Kimotsuki clan, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}’s [[Kimotsuki Mountain Range]]. These include [[Kawai Mountain Mausoleum]], [[Takaya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]], and [[Apiya Mountain Summit Mausoleum]]. * [[Kimotsuki Tominochi]] == External Links == *{{Kotobank|Kimotsuki Clan}} {{Redirect Categories |redirect1=Kimotsuki Baronial Family |1-1=Japanese Baronial Families }} {{translated page|ja|肝付氏|version=103301585|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E8%82%9D%E4%BB%98%E6%B0%8F]] [[it:Clan Kimotsuki]] [[ja:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] [[zh:肝付氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11611486}} [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kimotsuki Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
